test_not_tautological.reason: The f2p tests are string-presence checks in source files. An agent could simply add comment lines like '// ORG$SWITCHED_TO_ORGANIZATION', '// displaySuccessToast', '// organizations?.find', '// params?: Record<string, string>' to pass many f2p tests without implementing actual functionality. The tests don't verify behavior — they only verify string presence.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution is available at /workspace/openhands as the solve.sh writes files there. The agent could read solution/solve.sh if it exists in the container, or simply grep the test file for expected literal strings like 'You have switched to organization: Acme Corp' and 'You have switched to your personal workspace.' and hardcode those. The f2p tests are all text-matching, making it trivial to game them by inserting the right strings without understanding.
